name: Bagratid Armenia
note: Armenian and Georgian Bagratid dynasties rebuilt Christian statehood after the Arab conquest. The Armenian Bagratids (885-1045) and their Georgian branch created the 'Armenian Renaissance' with Ani as a capital that rivalled Constantinople. Ani fell to the Seljuk Turks in 1064, effectively ending Armenian political independence for centuries.

figures.name: Ashot I the Great
figures.significance: Acknowledged as King of Kings of Armenia by both Byzantine and Abbasid courts; restored Armenian independence after two centuries of Arab domination

figures.name: Gagik I
figures.significance: Greatest Bagratid king; Ani at its height was one of the world's great cities with 100,000 inhabitants and the Cathedral of Ani
